title: Tenant Service Clickthrough Policy Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the tenant service clickthrough policy capability, recovered from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage, not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facet descriptions are concise source-backed summaries citing verified manifest rows; proof is hermetic/stale, not current readiness. Enrich facet detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: Decides, per tenant and per service, whether a user may click through to an internal/composed service and derives the forward-auth allow sets plus the reverse-proxy (Caddyfile) reconciliation that enforce that decision at the edge. The capability is provider- and database-backed and owns tenant-affecting access policy, so its lifecycle/readiness model must be preserved and reconciliation kept consistent with the policy.

stateModel.description: Persisted state includes per-service descriptors (id, resource, apex path, tenant path, classification), a landing path, a dev-only flag, and the system-admin and tenant-admin resource sets, from which an access decision (granted plus reason) and a clickthrough URL are derived. State evolves through ordered, forward-only migrations that preserve the proven policy/decision model and cannot silently drop or remap tenant access rules.

permissions.description: Clickthrough decisions are the permission model: access is computed from system-admin and tenant-admin resource sets and the service classification, and the resulting forward-auth sets gate edge access per tenant. This is a security-sensitive surface where permission, audit, and fail-closed behaviour must be preserved; a service not explicitly granted is denied, and dev-only services are excluded outside the appropriate context.

contracts.description: Exposes an access-decision operation that returns granted/denied with a reason and a clickthrough URL, behind ports/adapters so the reverse-proxy reconciler is swappable without changing the policy contract. The derived forward-auth sets and reconciled proxy configuration are the contract's side effects, kept deterministic so the edge enforcement always matches the computed policy.

validation.description: Service descriptors, classification, and resource-set membership are validated before a decision is derived, and missing or invalid configuration fails closed rather than granting access by default. The dev-only flag and tenant/path bindings are checked so a malformed or out-of-context request cannot produce an over-broad clickthrough URL.

errorModel.description: Provider or reconciler failure returns a typed degraded/readiness failure and keeps mutation boundaries closed, never fabricating a granted decision or a successful reconciliation. Because access is tenant-affecting, a denied or indeterminate decision defaults to no access, and tenant data/policy loss is possible only if migration/restore semantics are bypassed.

auditModel.description: Access decisions and policy/reconciliation changes are recorded as auditable events before edge configuration is applied, preserving tenant boundary, the decision reason, and DSR/GDPR evidence. A missing tenant-isolation or audit signal on a clickthrough decision is an explicit alert condition.

readinessModel.description: Readiness reflects whether policy derivation and proxy reconciliation are healthy and degrades explicitly when the reconciler or substrate is unavailable, disabling reconciliation side-effects while keeping read-only readiness and audit visible. Emitted readiness, error, and audit signals use the controlled, OpenTelemetry-informed observability vocabulary.

proof.description: Behaviour is exercised by the cited service-clickthrough-policy and clickthrough runtime proofs plus service-clickthrough tests, demonstrating per-tenant access decisions and forward-auth/reconciliation derivation as hermetic/runtime lineage rather than a current-readiness claim. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; author the UI semantic model before generation.
